% Auto-generated by src/compute_human_entropy.py
% Reproducibility table: human response distribution + Shannon entropy.

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\small
\caption{Human overall response distribution by gender (pooled across all questions, mapped responses).}
\label{tab:human-gender-dist}
\begin{tabular}{lrrrrrrrrrrr}
\toprule
\textbf{Gender} & \textbf{n (responses)} & \textbf{A} & \textbf{B} & \textbf{C} & \textbf{D} & \textbf{E} & \textbf{F/G} & \textbf{$H$ (A--E)} & \textbf{$H_n$ (A--E)} & \textbf{$H$ (A--D)} & \textbf{$H_n$ (A--D)} \\
\midrule
Female & 1888 & 23.7\% & 24.3\% & 35.6\% & 11.5\% & 4.9\% & 1.8\% & 2.090 & 0.900 & 1.902 & 0.951 \\
Male & 1094 & 25.8\% & 26.9\% & 31.8\% & 11.4\% & 4.0\% & 2.6\% & 2.084 & 0.898 & 1.918 & 0.959 \\
Non-binary & 180 & 26.0\% & 24.3\% & 37.3\% & 9.0\% & 3.4\% & 1.7\% & 2.011 & 0.866 & 1.860 & 0.930 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\vspace{0.3em}
\begin{minipage}{0.95\linewidth}\scriptsize
$H$ = Shannon entropy in bits; $H_n = H / \log_2 k$ with $k=5$ for A--E and $k=4$ for the legacy A--D variant (matching \texttt{old\_results\_comparison.tex}). F/G (don't know / not applicable) reported as a single percentage and excluded from $H$. Percentages are over the A--E support. $n$ counts mapped responses, not respondents (each respondent answered $\sim$37 questions). Counts pooled across heterogeneous questions, so no per-question $k_q$ normalization is applied.
\end{minipage}
\end{table}
